channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: IEBC launches campaign to register 6 million voters ahead of 2027 elections
item.description: The Independent Electoral and Boundaries Commission (IEBC) has kicked off a major drive to register six million new voters ahead of Kenya's 2027 General Election. This initiative primarily targets the...

item.title: Australia maintains neutrality amid US-Iran conflict and Trump's remarks
item.description: US President Donald Trump has criticised allies, particularly the United Kingdom and France, for not participating in strikes against Iran, suggesting they should buy US oil and take control of the St...

item.title: President Ramaphosa to Open Sixth South Africa Investment Conference with Focus on Implementation and Growth
item.description: The Sixth South Africa Investment Conference (SAIC) is set to take place at the Sandton Convention Centre in Johannesburg, bringing together international investors, business leaders, and policymakers...

item.title: Pope Leo appeals to Trump to seek peaceful resolution to Iran conflict
item.description: In an unusual direct appeal, Pope Leo XIV urged US President Donald Trump to find an 'off-ramp' to end the ongoing war in Iran. Speaking outside his residence in Castel Gandolfo, Italy, the Pope highl...

item.title: Man Convicted After His XL Bully Dogs Attack and Kill Elderly Man
item.description: Sean Garner has been found guilty of owning a dangerously out of control dog after one of his XL bully dogs fatally mauled 84-year-old John McColl in February 2025. The incident occurred when Mr McCol...

item.title: Bennu Asteroid Samples Show Uneven Distribution of Organic Compounds and Minerals
item.description: A recent study of samples collected from asteroid Bennu by NASA's OSIRIS-REx mission reveals that the asteroid's chemistry is highly heterogeneous. Scientists found that organic compounds and minerals...

item.title: Tanzania Strengthens National ID System to Boost Services and Security
item.description: Tanzania is making big strides in modernising its national identification system, under the leadership of the National Identification Authority (NIDA). Over the years, NIDA has been improving citizen ...

item.title: Final Hours to Shop Amazon’s Big Spring Sale Deals Across Multiple Categories
item.description: Amazon's Big Spring Sale is offering significant discounts across a wide variety of product categories, but these deals are only available through the end of the day. CNN Underscored editors have high...

item.title: Esther Musila’s Family Clarifies Rumours About Estate and Supports Stepfather Guardian Angel
item.description: Kenyans on social media have been buzzing over claims about the estate left by the late businessman Erick Naibei, husband to gospel musician Esther Musila. Rumours suggested that Esther’s remarriage t...

item.title: Security Forces Conduct Successful Operations in KP, Killing 13 Militants
item.description: Security forces in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa conducted two separate intelligence-based operations on March 30, successfully neutralising 13 terrorists linked to the banned Tehreek-i-Taliban Pakistan, locally...

item.title: Trump Announces US Withdrawal from Iran Regardless of Deal Status
item.description: Former US President Donald Trump announced on April 1, 2026, that the United States is set to withdraw its forces from Iran very soon, regardless of whether a diplomatic deal is reached or not. The st...

item.title: North Bay police charge suspect with drug trafficking after foot chase
item.description: North Bay police responded to a situation that led to a foot pursuit and subsequent drug trafficking charges. The incident unfolded as officers attempted to apprehend a suspect involved in illegal dru...

item.title: Kenya Develops Mining Sector to Boost Investment, Jobs, and Industrial Growth
item.description: Kenya is now putting its mining sector at the centre of economic growth, foreign investment, and diplomacy. At the 19th Ambassadors' Conference in Nairobi, Principal Secretary Harry Kimtai outlined pl...

item.title: Artists Explore the Future of Luxury and Comfort in Lexus LS Concepts
item.description: Lexus has invited artists and designers to rethink the LS, shifting away from conventional automotive norms and toward a more human-centered vision of luxury. The resulting concepts focus on interior ...

item.title: Understanding Gen Z Slang: The Meaning of 'Slay'
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios, a leading media and entertainment company in the Philippines, highlights popular expressions among the younger generation, particularly Gen Z. Today’s focus is on the slang term 'slay...

item.title: Legendary April Fools' Prank Involved Fake Volcanic Eruption in Sitka, Alaska
item.description: On April 1, 1974, the quiet town of Sitka, Alaska, was momentarily thrown into panic when smoke appeared to rise from the dormant Hedgecumbe volcano. After more than four thousand years without activi...

item.title: Winnipeg Jets secure overtime victory against Chicago Blackhawks after losing lead
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets secured a hard-fought 4-3 overtime victory against the Chicago Blackhawks on Tuesday, closing in on a playoff spot in the Western Conference. Despite falling behind 1-0 in the first ...

item.title: Understanding the Unfinished Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain Caves at Ellora
item.description: The rock-cut caves at Ellora, renowned for their intricate sculptures and architectural splendour, include a significant number of unfinished monuments spanning Buddhist, Hindu, and Jain traditions. '...
